sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Confluence connector syncs spaces, pages, and blog posts from Confluence Cloud into the Shielva knowledge base using the Confluence REST API v2. Authentication is performed via HTTP Basic Auth with an Atlassian account email and API token. The connector implements cursor-based pagination (via _links.next), exponential backoff retry, and HTML stripping to produce clean indexable plain text from Confluence's storage format.

subsections.title: Atlassian API Token
subsections.content: All requests use HTTP Basic Auth: the email of your Atlassian account as the username, and an Atlassian API token as the password. API tokens are generated at id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens and do not expire unless manually revoked.
steps: Log in to id.atlassian.com and go to Security → API tokens.
Click Create API token and give it a descriptive label.
Copy the generated token — it is shown only once.
Enter your Confluence subdomain (e.g. 'mycompany' from mycompany.atlassian.net), your Atlassian account email, and the token in the Shielva connector install form.

fields.label: Confluence Domain
fields.description: Your Atlassian subdomain. If your URL is yourcompany.atlassian.net, enter 'yourcompany'. Do not include the protocol or .atlassian.net suffix.

fields.label: Atlassian Account Email
fields.description: The email address associated with the Atlassian account that owns the API token.

fields.label: API Token
fields.description: Atlassian API token from id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens. This is used as the Basic Auth password alongside your email.

sections.title: Sync Engine
sections.content: The sync() method performs a full paginated sync of all Confluence content accessible to the configured account. It first fetches all spaces, then for each space fetches all pages and blog posts using cursor-based pagination. Each raw API record is normalized into a ConnectorDocument with a stable SHA-256-derived ID and optionally ingested into the Shielva knowledge base.

methods.description: Validates credentials by probing GET /wiki/rest/api/user/current. Returns HEALTHY + CONNECTED on success, or OFFLINE + appropriate AuthStatus on failure.
Pings GET /wiki/rest/api/user/current. Returns HEALTHY, DEGRADED (transient network error), or OFFLINE (auth failure).
Full sync of all Confluence spaces, pages, and blog posts. Returns documents_found, documents_synced, documents_failed, and a SyncStatus (COMPLETED / PARTIAL / FAILED).
Returns the first page of Confluence spaces.

methods.signature: async def list_pages(space_id: str, limit: int = 50) -> dict
methods.description: Returns the first page of pages in a specific Confluence space.
Returns a single Confluence page with full body content in storage format.

methods.signature: async def list_blog_posts(space_id: str, limit: int = 50) -> dict
methods.description: Returns the first page of blog posts in a specific Confluence space.

methods.signature: async def search_content(query: str, limit: int = 50) -> dict
methods.description: CQL full-text search over all Confluence content accessible to the account.
Closes the underlying aiohttp.ClientSession and clears the http_client reference.

sections.title: Data Model
sections.description: Every Confluence content item is normalized into a ConnectorDocument before being ingested into the Shielva knowledge base.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Stable 16-hex-char ID: first 16 chars of SHA-256(page_id or post_id).

fields.name: content
fields.description: Newline-delimited plain text: title, space ID, author, timestamps, and stripped body text.

fields.name: source_url
fields.description: Deep link to the content in Confluence UI.

exceptions.name: ConfluenceError
exceptions.description: Base exception. Carries message, status_code, and code.

exceptions.name: ConfluenceAuthError
exceptions.description: Raised on 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden. Not retried.

exceptions.name: ConfluenceRateLimitError
exceptions.description: Raised on 429 Too Many Requests. Carries retry_after (seconds from Retry-After header). Retried after the specified delay.

exceptions.name: ConfluenceNotFoundError
exceptions.description: Raised on 404 Not Found. Carries resource name and resource ID.

exceptions.name: ConfluenceNetworkError
exceptions.description: Raised on aiohttp timeout or connection error. Retried with exponential backoff.

items.cause: API token is invalid, has been revoked, or the email is incorrect.
items.solution: Go to id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens, generate a new token, and update the connector's api_token field.

items.cause: The Atlassian account lacks view access to one or more Confluence spaces.
items.solution: Ask a Confluence admin to grant the account space viewer access, or use credentials for an account with broader access.
Verify you entered only the subdomain (e.g., 'mycompany' not 'mycompany.atlassian.net').
The connector retries automatically with exponential backoff. For very large Confluence instances, schedule syncs during off-peak hours.
Resolve the underlying network issue, then trigger a health check to verify recovery.

items.cause: One or more records returned unexpected data that caused normalization or fetch to fail.
items.solution: Check the connector logs for which space or page IDs failed. Common causes: private spaces with no access, corrupted page metadata.
